data.title: Cettire Says Citigroup Global Markets Australia Becomes Substantial Shareholder
data.description:
Cettire CTT said Citigroup Global Markets Australia became a substantial shareholder of the global online retailer on Tuesday, with a voting power of about 6.39%, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company's shares gained over 2% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Japan November household spending falls as price pressure persists
data.description:
Japanese household spending fell in November, government data showed on Friday, weighed down by stubbornly high prices that dissuaded consumers from loosening their purse strings.

Consumer spending dropped 0.4% in November from a year earlier, data from the internal affairs ministry showed, slightly better than the median market forecast for a 0.6% decline. On a seasonally adjusted, month-on-month basis, however, spending increased 0.4%, versus an expected 0.9% fall.

Consumers cut back spending on food, clothing and entertainment, while expenditure in education and housing went up, the data showed.

Consumption and wage trends are among key factors the Bank of Japan (BOJ) is watching to gauge the strength of Japan's economy and decide how soon to raise interest rates.

November's pay data released on Thursday showed inflation-adjusted wages slid for the fourth straight month in November, dragged by higher prices even though base pay grew at the fastest pace in three decades.

The BOJ ended massive monetary stimulus and raised interest rates to 0.25% last year. While some investors are betting the next rate hike will take place at a Jan. 23-24 meeting, others see a stronger chance in March.

data.title: Light & Wonder Names Digital Gaming Business Head
data.description:
Light & Wonder LNW appointed its international managing director Simon Johnson as the chief executive of the iGaming business, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Johnson will transition from the company's gaming division to spearhead its digital gaming business, the filing said.

data.title: JAPAN NOV HOUSEHOLD SPENDING DATA ALSO SHOWS PULLBACKS IN AIR CONDITIONERS/WASHERS, GOLF FEES AS COLD WEATHER REPLACED UNUSUALLY LONG HEAT WAVE
Peru Lower Key Rate by 25 bps to 4.75%
data.description:
Peru’s central bank reduced its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 4.75% during its January 2025 meeting, marking the lowest level since April 2022.

This move follows a series of rate cuts, now totaling 300 basis points, that began in September 2023.

In its statement, the board noted that global inflation is expected to slow toward the respective goals of most central banks, and in the case of some countries these goals have already been achieved.

Domestic inflation also slowed to 2% in December from 2.3% in November, right at the center of the target range.

Meanwhile, the bank highlighted signs of slowing economic activity, although the outlook remains optimistic.

Looking ahead, the board remains especially attentive to new information regarding inflation and its determinants, reaffirming its commitment to adopt the necessary actions to maintain inflation within the target range.

data.title: Australia's Radiopharm Theranostics jumps on securing premium placement from US co Lantheus
data.description:
** Radiopharm Theranostics RAD leaps as much as 50% to A$0.036; set for biggest ever intraday pct gain

** Biopharma secures private placement agreement with Massachusetts-based Lantheus Holdings LNTH to raise $5 mln

** Issue price of A$0.060 represents 150% premium to last close

** Co earlier signed co-development deal with the NASDAQ-listed firm to develop biopharmaceuticals in Australia

** Stock hits its highest level since Dec. 10, 2024

** More than 12.9 mln shares traded, 1.2x the 30-day avg

** RAD fell 67.6% in 2024 - third consecutive bear run since 2022

data.title: Australia's Structural Monitoring Systems jumps on premium placement
data.description:
** Structural Monitoring Systems SSMNOA gains 4.3% to A$0.490, highest since Dec. 27, 2024

** Electronic products manufacturer gets commitments for A$1.337 mln ($828,405.20) through single-tranche placement

** Issue price of A$0.52 represents 10.6% premium to last close

** Co says proceeds will be used for product development and manufacturing capacity expansion

** Stock fell ~4% last year

($1 = A$1.6139)

Helmerich & Payne, Inc. Announces Fiscal First Quarter 2025 Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
Helmerich & Payne, Inc. Announces Fiscal First Quarter 2025 Conference Call and Webcast

TULSA, Okla.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--January 09, 2025--

In conjunction with Helmerich & Payne, Inc.'s HP fiscal first quarter 2025 earnings release, you are invited to listen to its conference call on Thursday, February 06, 2025, at 11:00 a.m. (ET) with John Lindsay, President and CEO, Kevin Vann, Senior Vice President and CFO, and Dave Wilson, Vice President of Investor Relations. The earnings release and presentation for the quarterly results will be available on the company's website at www.helmerichpayne.com. Investors may listen to the conference call either by phone or audio webcast.

 
  What:              Helmerich & Payne, Inc.'s Fiscal First Quarter 2025 
                     Earnings Release. Other material developments may also be 
                     discussed. 
 
  When:              11:00 a.m. ET (10:00 a.m. CT), Thursday, February 06, 
                     2025 
 
  Via Phone:         Domestic: 800-445-7795 Access Code: Helmerich 
                     International: 785-424-1699 Access Code: Helmerich 
 
  Via Internet:      Visit http://www.helmerichpayne.com then click on 
                     "Investors" and then click on "News & Events -- Event & 
                     Presentations" to find the link to the webcast. 
 
  Questions:         Dave Wilson, investor.relations@hpinc.com, 918-588-5190

If you are unable to listen during the live webcast, the call will be archived for 365 days on Helmerich & Payne, Inc.'s website, http://www.helmerichpayne.com, under "News & Events — Event & Presentations," which can be accessed through the "Investors" section of the website.

About Helmerich & Payne, Inc.

Founded in 1920, Helmerich & Payne, Inc. is committed to delivering industry leading drilling productivity and reliability. H&P operates with the highest level of integrity, safety and innovation to deliver superior results for our customers and returns for shareholders. Through its subsidiaries, the Company designs, fabricates and operates high-performance drilling rigs in conventional and unconventional plays around the world. H&P also develops and implements advanced automation, directional drilling and survey management technologies. For more information, visit www.helmerichpayne.com.

Helmerich & Payne uses its website as a channel of distribution for material company information. Such information is routinely posted and accessible on its investor relations website at www.helmerichpayne.com.

CONTACT: Dave Wilson

investor.relations@hpinc.com

918-588-5190

data.title: University of Maryland Medical System to Receive $301 Million From Bond Sale
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

The Maryland Health and Higher Educational Facilities Authority plans to sell $301 million of municipal bonds for the University of Maryland Medical System.

The sale will include $227.9 million of Series 2025A bonds, encompassing serial bonds due from 2025 through 2042, and a $143 million term bond due in 2052, according to an offering statement posted Wednesday on MuniOS. The authority will sell $73 million of Series 2025B bonds maturing in 2045.

Delivery is expected on or about Feb. 12.

The authority also expects to sell $150 million of Series 2025C bonds in February.

The bonds are special obligations of the authority payable solely from payments by University of Maryland Medical System and certain affiliates to the authority or its trustee.

Money from sale will be used to help pay for construction of the Shore Regional Medical Center in Easton, the Center for Advanced Medicine project in Baltimore and refund other securities, according to the preliminary official statement. The medical system has more than 28,000 employees operating in over 150 locations, including 10 acute care hospitals and five freestanding medical facilities.

Bond counsel believes interest isn't an item of preference for purposes of the alternative minimum tax for individuals.

The bonds have ratings of A2 from Moody's Ratings rated the bonds A2, and S&P Global Ratings has them rated at A.

Lead underwriters are Morgan Stanley and RBC Capital Markets.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Manulife US REIT: In Talks to Sell Multiple Properties to Deleverage Balance Sheet
Disco's Non-consolidated Net Sales Gain 18% in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Semiconductor firm Disco 6146 said non-consolidated net sales gained 18.1% year on year to 77.4 billion yen, in the fiscal third quarter, according to a bourse filing on Thursday.

For the first three quarters, net sales inched up 3.1% after equipment sales, including precision processing, were recorded upon customer inspection or acceptance.

Non-consolidated net shipments, which better reflect market trends, grew 37.1% on year in the third quarter to 90.8 billion yen.

Precision processing equipment shipments stayed steady due to generative AI demand, while consumables demand remained high.

data.title: Market Chatter: Star Entertainment Agrees to Sell Luxury Yacht at Center of Legal Dispute; Shares Hit All-Time Low
data.description:
Star Entertainment Group SGR agreed to sell a luxury yacht to Platinum Capital Partners after allegedly reneging on the deal, settling a legal dispute involving the already struggling casino operator, the Australian Financial Review reported Thursday.

Platinum, the private investment vehicle of Roys Poyiadjis and his family, alleged that Star Entertainment had agreed to sell the yacht in February and backed out of the deal after receiving a deposit and other fees.

Platinum's claim, lodged in April 2024, was discontinued on Jan. 7, the report said, citing the court.

Star Entertainment did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Star Entertainment Group's shares tumbled almost 14% in recent Friday trade and earlier hit an all-time low.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

AnyMind Becomes Exclusive Distributor for SUNA in Vietnam
data.description:
AnyMind Group 5027 has been appointed the exclusive online distributor for SUNA, NIL Corporation's premium Japanese haircare brand, in Vietnam, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

SUNA, known for its advanced scalp care solutions, will utilize AnyMind's technology platforms to manage its e-commerce operations. This follows AnyMind's role as SUNA's exclusive distributor in Thailand and Malaysia.

The partnership leverages AnyMind's BPaaS solutions, including store management, influencer marketing, and Gen AI-powered live streaming, to drive sales and enhance consumer engagement in the Vietnamese market.

data.title: Japan Personal Spending Falls the Least in 4 Months
data.description:
Japan's household spending dropped by 0.4% yoy in November 2024, below market expectations of a 0.6% drop and after a 1.3% decline in the previous month.

It was the fourth consecutive month of decrease but the softest in the sequence.

data.title: JAPAN NOV REAL CORE HOUSEHOLD SPENDING (EX-HOUSING, VEHICLES, GIFT MONEY) -1.9% Y/Y VS. -1.0% IN OCT WHEN OVERALL SPENDING FELL 1.3%
Cube's All-Store Sales Jump 22% in December 2024
data.description:
Cube's 7112 all stores sales jumped 22% in December 2024, bringing its full-year sales growth to 3.5% year on year, according to its Thursday filing.

For existing stores, December sales jumped 9.7% but for the full year, sales slipped 4%.

The company's e-commerce sales for all stores recorded an increase of 25.7%, while physical store sales grew 20% on year in December.

For the full year, all stores' e-commerce sales fell 6.5% but physical stores gained 9.9%.

Mesoblast Requests Trading Halt
data.description:
Mesoblast MSB requested that its securities be placed in trading halt until an announcement related to a proposed financing is made, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The trading halt will remain in place until the start of trading on Jan. 14 or when the company makes the financing announcement, the filing said.

data.title: Nikkei May Decline as Concerns About Borrowing Costs Continue — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks may decline as concerns about higher borrowing costs continue. Nikkei futures are down 0.3% at 39460 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 158.09, compared with 158.14 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any comments on the yen's recent depreciation from Japanese government officials as well as any developments over U.S.-China trade relations. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 0.9% to 39605.09 on Thursday.(kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1840 ET - Japanese stocks may decline as concerns about higher borrowing costs continue. Nikkei futures are down 0.3% at 39460 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 158.09, compared with 158.14 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any comments on the yen's recent depreciation from Japanese government officials as well as any developments over U.S.-China trade relations. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 0.9% to 39605.09 on Thursday.(kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1633 ET - Fed governor Michelle Bowman tells a bankers' conference in Laguna Beach, Calif. that when she supported a quarter-point rate cut in December, she saw it as the last bit of easing needed to bring the Fed's policy stance into alignment. "I supported the December policy action because, in my view, it represented the Committee's final step in the policy recalibration phase," Bowman says in a published text of her remarks. Even so, the rate cut was a close call, she says: "Given the lack of continued progress on lowering inflation and the ongoing strength in economic activity and in the labor market, I could have supported taking no action at the December meeting." Bowman's dissent against September's half-point rate cut was the first by a Fed governor since 2005. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1627 ET - Minutes of the Bank of Mexico's December policy meeting show three of the five board members willing to increase the size of interest-rate cuts to 50 bps, but whether that's possible in February will depend on January inflation and the peso's reaction to any policy announcements by President-elect Trump when he takes office, Barclays analysts Gabriel Casillas and Erick Martinez say in a note. They also see a more hawkish Fed as a "binding constraint" on the Bank of Mexico. "We continue to expect another 25bp rate cut at its next meeting," they add, "and then the possibility of being able to step up the pace of cuts to 50bp, taking the reference rate to 8.50% by mid-2025."(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1523 ET - Treasury yields fall in a shortened session to honor former President Jimmy Carter. For tomorrow, economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast nonfarm payrolls of 155,000, down from November's 227,000. Unemployment is expected to remain at 4.2% and average hourly earnings to growth 4% over a year earlier, the same pace as in November. If the data points to a hotter-than-expected labor market, it could trigger bets on a more hawkish Fed, potentially boosting yields. If too cold, they could indicate the Fed won't be able to wait much longer for further cuts, weakening yields. The 10-year falls 0.011 percentage point to 4.680% and the two-year is down 0.027 p.p. to 4.261%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1442 ET - Industrial production in Mexico likely remained slow in November. Industrial output is seen edging up 0.1% from October in seasonally adjusted terms, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts. Production is expected to be down 1.6% unadjusted from November of 2023. Industrial production slowed significantly in the first 10 months of 2024, with output up 0.7% in the January-October period, compared with 3.4% growth in all of 2023. Statistics institute Inegi is scheduled to report November industrial production on Friday. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1323 ET - President-elect Trump's call for allies to spend 5% of national GDP on defense would make the hemisphere less safe, less secure and more vulnerable, Scotiabank's Derek Holt argues. Canada currently spends about 1.25%, or C$40 billion annually. Raising that to the 2% NATO commitment would equal C$60 billion a year if implemented overnight, though Canada has pledged to reach the target by 2032, Holt calculates. He estimates that to hit 5% of GDP would mean a whopping C$150 billion a year if done overnight or C$210 billion annually if implemented by 2032, raising the total over a decade to between C$1.5 trillion and C$2 trillion for a roughly C$3 trillion economy. Holt says that ain't gonna happen, and certainly not if Canada faces crushing tariffs and the country's deficit blows out, leaving even less room for defense. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

1219 ET - The increase in U.K. bond yields to levels not seen since 1998 will boost U.K. pension providers' solvency by increasing the discount applied to personal liabilities, says Mark Capleton, an analyst at BofA Securities. "This should be generally speaking quite favorable for pension schemes," Capleton says. The yield on the 30-year bond or gilt, as they are called in the U.K., hit 5.455% earlier Thursday, while the yield on the 10-year gilt rose to 4.921%, before stabilizing in afternoon trading. The selloff has been more gradual than in October 2022, when the long-term bond yields rose on the back of the U.K. mini-budget under the leadership of former U.K. Prime Minister Liz Truss, Capleton says. Market liquidity is much more normal nowadays and the daily moves are more modest, he adds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

1201 ET - Canada's largest business lobby says it is "inappropriate" for the country's tax-collection authority to enforce higher capital-gains taxes when the Liberal government failed to pass implementing legislation. Outgoing PM Justin Trudeau dissolved parliament this week when he announced his resignation--and that means all pieces of legislation seeking approval, including on higher capital-gain taxes, are expunged. The Canadian Chamber of Commerce says it wants the Liberal government to order tax collectors to cease collecting higher capital-gains taxes until after the election that is mandated this year, "if at all." Canada's Department of Finance says the tax collection will proceed, citing parliamentary convention. The chamber says uncertainty about the tax measure is driving away investment. The Conservative Party, which has a large lead in public-opinion polls, has opposed the tax change. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com)

1159 ET - Capital Economics predicts Canada GDP could fall 3% and the Canadian dollar decline by 10% in the event President-elect Donald Trump slaps a 25% tariff on all Canadian imports. Stephen Brown, an economist at the forecasting firm, says CAD would need to weaken even further--USDCAD at 1.78, from its current 1.44 level--to totally offset the 25% tariff hit. Brown believes the Bank of Canada would not allow CAD to weaken that much, due to the impact on inflation. Conversely, Canada could withstand the fallout from the universal 10% tariff that Trump has championed, Brown says, as that levy would apply across the globe. A 25% tariff for Canada, though, would likely force US buyers to replace Canadian goods and services with substitutes from elsewhere. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1157 ET - Any potential positive for the Canadian dollar from new political leadership are likely to overwhelmed by poor domestic fundamentals U.S. tariff threats, argues Barclay's' Skylar Montgomery Koning. Any C$ bounces are likely to prove short lived, she says. Her research suggests President-elect Trump's threatened 25% tariff on all imports from Canada--seemingly his attempt to tackle Canada's large trade surplus with its neighbor--would, if enacted, lead to a 19% C$ depreciation against the dollar and take USD/CAD to 1.63, based on an import-substitution model. As it stands, Canada's government is rudderless and vulnerable to threats as Trump takes office, with parliament prorogued until late March and an election campaign still to be held. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

1112 ET - The Mid-Atlantic and Florida offer the best opportunities for first-time homebuyers, a Realtor.com survey says. Harrisburg, Penn., tops the list, followed by Rochester, N.Y., and several Florida cities including Villas, Lauderdale Lakes, and Altamonte Springs. These markets were selected for affordable listings, robust local economies, family-friendly environments, and investment potential. With national asking rents declining for 16 months, buying has become more appealing, especially where housing costs 30% or less of income. In these top markets, housing expenses range from 15.2% of prospective homebuyers' income in Rochester to 24.8% in Wilmington, with median listing prices below the national median of $416,880. The most expensive market is Villas, Fla., which has a median listing price of $236,950. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1038 ET - U.S. median asking rent dropped 0.3% year-over-year to $1,594 in December, the lowest since March 2022, according to Redfin. This is down 0.1% from November and 6.2% from the August 2022 peak of $1,700. The median asking rent per square foot, decreased 1.9% annually to $1.78. The decline is linked to a surge in apartment supply, with completions up 58.1% year-over-year, leading to an 8% vacancy rate. Specifically, median asking rents for 0-1 bedroom units fell 1% to $1,449, 2 bedrooms by 0.6% to $1,665, and 3+ bedrooms by 2.5% to $1,950.(chris.wack@wsj.com)

data.title: Indonesia Motorbike Sales Fall 5.5% in December
data.description:
Motorbike sales in Indonesia dropped 5.5% year-on-year to 403,480 units in December 2024, after a 10.3% plunge in the previous month, marking the second straight month of decline.

For the full year of 2024, sales grew by 1.5%, reaching 6.3 million units, within the Association of Indonesia Motorcycle Industry (AISI) forecast range of 6.2 to 6.5 million units.

In 2023, motorbike sales jumped by 19.4% to 6.2 million units, within AISI’s target range of 6.1 to 6.2 million units.

On a monthly basis, motorbike sales slumped 21.3%, following a 5.8% decline in November.

data.title: Weimob: Shareholder Tencent Holdings Sold About 5.45% of Company Stake in Open Market
Toyoda Gosei Says Its GaN Substrate Tech 'Enhances' Power Device Performance
data.description:
Toyoda Gosei's 7282 GaN substrate technology, developed with Osaka University and supported by Japan's Ministry of the Environment, has shown significant improvements in power device performance, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Published in Physica Status Solidi, the study reveals that GaN devices fabricated on Toyoda Gosei's substrates outperform those on commercially available ones, offering better power regulation and higher yield ratios.

The advanced substrates, with minimal property variability, support more efficient and energy-saving power devices essential for CO2 reduction.

Toyoda Gosei plans to continue refining the technology and collaborating to accelerate large-scale production of high-quality GaN substrates.

data.title: FendX Technologies to Present at The Microcap Conference 2025 in Atlantic City, NJ
data.description:
OAKVILLE, ON / ACCESSWIRE / January 9, 2025 / FendX Technologies Inc.FNDX(OTCQB:FDXTF)(FSE:E8D) (the"Company" or "FendX"), a company developing products to protect surfaces and reduce the spread of pathogens, is pleased to announce its participation in The Microcap Conference 2025, the premier event for growth-focused companies and investors. The conference will take place January 28-30, 2025, at the Borgata Hotel Spa & Casino in Atlantic City, NJ.

FendX's management team will deliver a corporate presentation and engage in one-on-one meetings with institutional and individual investors to discuss the company's recent developments, growth strategy, and investment opportunities.

About The Microcap Conference 2025

The Microcap Conference is the largest independent microcap event in the U.S., bringing together top-tier investors and executives from microcap companies. The event offers a platform for companies to showcase their value propositions through presentations, one-on-one meetings, and networking opportunities.

The 2025 event will feature:

  • Keynote Speakers: Renowned industry figures, including Jon Ledecky, Co-Owner of the New York Islanders, who will engage in a fireside chat with CNBC's Bob Pisani; plus Tom Gardner, CEO of Motley Fool, who will share insights on investing, market trends, and entrepreneurial success.

  • Expert Panels and Presentations: Financial commentators Ron Insana (CNBC) and Charlie Gasparino (FOX Business) will cover critical topics for the US equity markets, from capital formation to regulatory updates and market trends.

  • Entertainment Headliner: A special performance by Tom Papa, celebrated comedian and host of Netflix specials, ensuring a memorable evening for attendees.

Hosted by DealFlow Events, The Microcap Conference is renowned for its blend of high-quality content, engaging networking, and exceptional entertainment. For more information, visit https://themicrocapconference.com.

About FendX Technologies Inc.

At the intersection of healthcare and advanced materials, FendX is dedicated to advancing products under development using its innovative nanotechnology to keep surfaces safe from contamination. Products in development include a film and nano-particle solution that have been shown to significantly reduce the adhesion and transmission of bacteria and viruses. REPELWRAP™ film is currently in real-world beta-testing and the solution is in the formulation phase. FendX is also using its novel nanotechnology to develop nano-coated catheter protypes to minimize blood clots and bacterial biofilm formation. The Company conducts R&D in collaboration with industry-leading partners, including McMaster University, and has exclusive worldwide licenses to its nanotechnology from McMaster. FendX is also actively pursuing additional in-licensing and acquisition opportunities to expand its infection disease control product portfolio and enhance its impact in protecting surfaces.

Media Contact:

Dr. Carolyn Myers

CEO

FendX Technologies Inc.

1-800-344-9868

investor@fendxtech.com

For more information, please visit https://fendxtech.com/ and the Company's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

Neither the Canadian Securities Exchange nor the Market Regulator (as that term is defined in the policies of the Canadian Securities Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Forward-Looking Statements

This news release contains certain forward-looking statements within the meaning of Canadian securities legislation, including with respect to: the plans of the Company; and products under development and any pathogen reduction benefits related thereto. Although the Company believes that such statements are reasonable, it can give no assurance that such expectations will prove to be correct. The Company cautions that forward-looking statements are based on the beliefs, estimates and opinions of the Company's management on the date the statements are made and involve several risks and uncertainties. Consequently, there can be no assurances that such statements will prove to be accurate, and that actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements.

Important factors that could cause future results to differ materially from those anticipated in these forward-looking statements include: product candidates only being in formulation/reformulation stages; limited operating history; research and development activities; dependence on collaborative partners, licensors and others; effect of general economic and political conditions; and other risk factors set forth in the Company's public filings which are available on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.
